---
title: "Excessive Optimism"
categories: [heuristics, overconfidence]
---

This bias describes the tendency to believe that good outcomes of events outside our control are more likely than bad outcomes, despite what objective probabilities might suggest. It differs from [overconfidence](overconfidence.qmd), which concerns events we think we influence.

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse="false"}
## Example

#### Entering new markets

Investing in unfamiliar markets after a brief review period without adequate risk assessment or professional advice.

!["We'll read financial news for a week, and then invest in ETFs."](../images/excessive-optimism.png){width="300px" fig-align="center"}

::: {.also-relates}
**Also relates to:** [Overconfidence](overconfidence.qmd) · [Illusion of Control](illusion-of-control.qmd) · [Extrapolation Bias](extrapolation-bias.qmd) · [Hyperbolic Discounting](hyperbolic-discounting.qmd)
:::

:::
